interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, mission-driven (wellness), calm pace

coding: Given an array of integers, find the two numbers that add up to a target value. Talk through your approach, complexity, and edge cases.
Given a string, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters. Walk through your solution.
Design a data structure that supports insert, delete, and get-random in constant time. Explain your reasoning.
behavioral: Tell me about a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Describe a project that failed or fell short. What did you learn and change afterward?
Give me an example of when you took ownership of something outside your job description.
other: How does Calm compare to Headspace?

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Calm
calm.com ↗

Calm is the leading meditation and mental health app — 100M+ downloads. The interview emphasizes mobile content delivery, audio streaming, subscription management, and the unique design considerations of a wellness product.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design audio content delivery with offline downloads

- Design subscription management and family plans

- Design personalization for content recommendations

- Coding: medium DSA, often with mobile or content framing

- Behavioral: customer focus, mission-driven (wellness), calm pace

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $150K–$190K total

- Senior SE: $215K–$285K

- Staff: $310K–$410K

- Principal: $430K–$570K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Swift / Kotlin (mobile) and Node.js (backend)

- Understand audio streaming and offline downloads

- Brush up on subscription billing patterns

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Calm remote-friendly?

Distributed across San Francisco, NYC, London. Most engineering roles fully remote within US.

How does Calm compare to Headspace?

Calm has slightly larger user base. Headspace is more clinical / therapy-integrated. Comp is comparable.

What is the engineering culture?

Mission-driven (wellness), calm pace, strong work-life balance. Engineers who believe in the product thrive.
